quality.impact: Without explicit path, FLATTEN depends on column position which can silently produce wrong results after schema changes.
quality.fix_guidance: Use explicit parameters: LATERAL FLATTEN(input => col, path => 'key').

quality.name: INSERT Without Column List
quality.description: INSERT INTO table VALUES (...) without specifying columns depends on column order. If the table schema changes (column added, reordered), the INSERT silently inserts data into wrong columns or fails.
quality.impact: A schema change (ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN) silently shifts all values one position, causing data corruption without any error.
quality.fix_guidance: Always specify columns: INSERT INTO table (col1, col2) VALUES (v1, v2).

quality.name: CASE Without ELSE
quality.description: A CASE expression without ELSE returns NULL when no condition matches. This is a frequent source of unexpected NULLs that propagate through calculations and break NOT NULL constraints.
quality.impact: Unmatched CASE returns NULL, which propagates through arithmetic (NULL + 1 = NULL), string operations (NULL || 'x' = NULL), and comparisons (NULL = NULL is UNKNOWN).
quality.fix_guidance: Always add ELSE with a sensible default: CASE WHEN x THEN y ELSE default_value END.

quality.name: Deprecated LOCK IN SHARE MODE
quality.description: LOCK IN SHARE MODE is deprecated in MySQL 8.0 in favor of FOR SHARE. The old syntax still works but may be removed in future versions.
quality.impact: Code using LOCK IN SHARE MODE will break when the syntax is removed in a future MySQL version.
quality.fix_guidance: Replace LOCK IN SHARE MODE with FOR SHARE (MySQL 8.0+).

quality.name: SET QUOTED_IDENTIFIER OFF
quality.description: SET QUOTED_IDENTIFIER OFF allows double quotes to delimit strings instead of identifiers. This is non-standard, deprecated, and breaks indexed views, computed columns, and filtered indexes.
quality.impact: With QUOTED_IDENTIFIER OFF, indexed views and computed columns cannot be created or queried. This is deprecated and will be removed in a future SQL Server version.
quality.fix_guidance: Remove SET QUOTED_IDENTIFIER OFF. Use single quotes for strings and double quotes or square brackets for identifiers.

cost.name: Full Table Scan on Large Tables
cost.description: Detects queries that likely trigger full table scans by lacking WHERE clauses on SELECT statements. Full scans consume excessive compute and I/O credits in cloud databases.
cost.impact: Full table scans linearly increase compute cost with table size. On cloud databases (AWS RDS, Azure SQL, GCP CloudSQL), this wastes IOPS and CPU credits, especially on large tables.
cost.fix_guidance: Add a WHERE clause to filter rows. If a full scan is truly needed, consider using a separate analytics replica or data warehouse (e.g., BigQuery, Redshift) to avoid impacting OLTP workloads and costs.

cost.name: Expensive Window Functions Without Partitioning
cost.description: Detects window functions (ROW_NUMBER, RANK, DENSE_RANK, LAG, LEAD, etc.) used without PARTITION BY. Without partitioning, the entire dataset is processed as one partition, increasing memory and compute costs.

cost.name: SELECT * in ETL/CTAS Queries
cost.description: Detects SELECT * in CREATE TABLE AS SELECT (CTAS), INSERT INTO ... SELECT, or other data persistence patterns. This copies unnecessary columns into storage, inflating storage and backup costs.

cost.name: Redundant ORDER BY in Subqueries
cost.description: Detects ORDER BY clauses inside subqueries where the outer query re-sorts or doesn't use ordering. Sorting is expensive (disk I/O for temp tables) and wasteful if results are re-sorted.

cost.name: Cross-Region Data Transfer
cost.description: Flags queries using database links, federated queries, or external table references that may cause cross-region data transfer. Cloud providers charge heavily for egress traffic.
cost.impact: Cross-region queries incur data egress charges (e.g., $0.09/GB in AWS). A single unoptimized federated query can transfer terabytes and generate unexpected bills.
cost.fix_guidance: Minimize cross-region queries. Use data replication (read replicas, CDC) or cache results locally. For analytics, stage data in the same region as compute resources.

cost.name: OFFSET Pagination Without Index
cost.description: Detects OFFSET-based pagination on non-indexed columns. In SQL, OFFSET forces the database to scan and discard rows, becoming exponentially slower and more expensive on later pages.
cost.impact: OFFSET 10000 forces the database to scan and discard 10,000 rows. On page 1000, you pay for scanning 1 million rows. In cloud databases, this means IOPS charges for wasted work.
cost.fix_guidance: Use keyset/cursor pagination: WHERE id > last_seen_id ORDER BY id LIMIT 100. This maintains constant cost per page. For random access, use search indexing.

cost.name: Deep Pagination Without Cursor
cost.description: Detects OFFSET values >1000, indicating deep pagination that should use a cursor/keyset approach for better performance and lower cost.
cost.impact: Deep pagination (OFFSET > 1000) means scanning thousands of rows per page. Cloud databases charge per row scanned. Users on page 100+ generate 100x more cost than page 1 users.
cost.fix_guidance: Implement cursor-based pagination: return cursor token with last record ID. Next page: WHERE id > cursor ORDER BY id LIMIT 100.

cost.name: COUNT(*) for Pagination Total
cost.description: Detects COUNT(*) queries used to calculate total pages, which can be expensive on large tables and is often unnecessary for user experience.
cost.impact: COUNT(*) on large tables requires full table scan or index scan. For 100M row table, this can take 30+ seconds and cost significant IOPS. Users rarely navigate past page 3.
cost.fix_guidance: Avoid showing total counts beyond page 10. Use approximate counts or cached counts updated periodically. Show 'More results' instead of page numbers.

cost.name: Duplicate Index Signal
cost.description: Detects CREATE INDEX statements which may duplicate existing indexes (same columns, different name). Duplicate indexes waste storage and slow down writes.
cost.impact: Duplicate indexes waste storage (each index = 100% of indexed data), slow down writes (every INSERT/UPDATE maintains all indexes), and cost money in cloud storage charges.
cost.fix_guidance: Query system catalog to find duplicates (e.g., pg_indexes). Keep only the most selective index. Use covering indexes instead of multiple single-column indexes.

cost.name: Over-Indexed Table Signal
cost.description: Flags CREATE INDEX on tables that likely already have many indexes, causing massive write penalties and increased cloud storage costs.
cost.impact: Tables with 10+ indexes pay massive write penalties. Each INSERT updates all indexes. Write throughput can drop 90%. Cloud databases charge for IOPS consumed by index maintenance.
cost.fix_guidance: Audit index usage and drop unused indexes. Consolidate into composite or covering indexes.

cost.name: Missing Covering Index Opportunity
cost.description: Detects SELECT with WHERE filters and specific columns that could benefit from a covering index, eliminating expensive table lookups.
cost.impact: Non-covering indexes require key lookup - reading index then reading table. Covering indexes eliminate table access, reducing I/O by 50-90%.
cost.fix_guidance: Create covering index: CREATE INDEX idx_name ON table(where_cols) INCLUDE (select_cols). Monitor index size vs benefit.

cost.name: Redundant Index Column Order
cost.description: Detects composite index creation where column order may not match common query patterns, leading to wasted indexes and slower queries.
cost.impact: Index (col_B, col_A) cannot optimize WHERE col_A = ?. Column order matters. Wrong order = wasted index and slower queries.
cost.fix_guidance: Order index columns by selectivity and query usage. For queries filtering col_A then col_B, use INDEX(col_A, col_B).

cost.name: Cross-Database JOIN
cost.description: Detects JOIN across different databases, which forces data transfer and prevents query optimization.
cost.impact: Cross-database JOINs cannot use indexes across boundaries. Forces full table scans and data copying. In cloud, this means egress charges and 10-100x slower queries.
cost.fix_guidance: Denormalize data into single database or use ETL to replicate needed data. Consider microservices with API calls instead of cross-DB queries.

cost.name: Multi-Region Query Latency
cost.description: Detects queries using database links, federated tables, or region qualifiers indicating cross-region data access.
cost.impact: Cross-region queries add 50-200ms latency per request. Egress charges of $0.02-0.12/GB also apply.
cost.fix_guidance: Use read replicas in each region or implement a caching layer (Redis) for cross-region reads.

cost.name: Distributed Transaction Overhead
cost.description: Detects distributed transaction patterns (BEGIN DISTRIBUTED TRANSACTION, XA START) that are 10-100x slower than local transactions.
cost.impact: Distributed transactions require 2-phase commit across nodes, holding locks for network round-trips. Throughput drops significantly.
cost.fix_guidance: Avoid distributed transactions. Use Saga pattern for cross-service consistency. Implement compensating transactions or eventual consistency.

cost.name: Cold Start Query Pattern
cost.description: Detects complex queries in serverless environments (Aurora Serverless) that will trigger cold starts and ACU scaling, increasing costs.
cost.impact: Complex queries trigger Aurora Capacity Unit (ACU) scaling. Each scale-up costs minimum $0.12/hour. Frequent scaling wastes budget.
cost.fix_guidance: Keep queries simple in serverless. Pre-aggregate data or use materialized views. Set minimum ACUs appropriately.

cost.name: Unnecessary Connection Pooling
cost.description: Detects connection management patterns that are wasteful in serverless (connections held open unnecessarily).
cost.impact: Serverless databases charge per second of connection time. Keeping connections alive between invocations wastes money.
cost.fix_guidance: Close connections immediately after query in Lambda/serverless. Use RDS Proxy for connection pooling.

cost.name: Old Data Not Archived
cost.description: Detects SELECT on tables with date columns, suggesting potential for archival of old data to reduce hot storage costs.
cost.impact: Storing years of logs in hot storage costs 10x vs cold storage (S3 Glacier). Old data wastes IOPS and backup capacity.
cost.fix_guidance: Implement data lifecycle: archive data > 90 days old to S3/Glacier. Use table partitioning by date.

cost.name: Large Text Column Without Compression
cost.description: Detects CREATE TABLE with large VARCHAR/TEXT columns that should use compression to save storage costs.
cost.impact: Uncompressed TEXT columns waste 3-10x storage space. Cloud storage charges are significant for uncompressed data.
cost.fix_guidance: Enable row/page compression (e.g., ROW_FORMAT=COMPRESSED in MySQL). Use JSONB instead of TEXT for JSON data.

cost.name: Large Table Without Partitioning
cost.description: Detects queries on large tables without partition pruning signals, which can be extremely expensive on large datasets.
cost.impact: Scanning unpartitioned 1B row table costs 100x more than scanning one partition. Partitioning by date reduces cost by 90-99% for time-range queries.
cost.fix_guidance: Partition large tables by date. Most queries filter by date - partition pruning eliminates 99% of data.

cost.name: SELECT * in BigQuery Scans All Columns
cost.impact: BigQuery charges per byte scanned. SELECT * on a 1TB table with 50 columns costs 50x more than selecting only the 1 column you need. At $5/TB, a daily job doing SELECT * can cost thousands per month unnecessarily.
cost.fix_guidance: Always specify only the columns you need. Use column pruning: SELECT id, name, date FROM table. Partition and cluster tables on frequently filtered columns to reduce scan cost.

cost.name: BigQuery Query Without LIMIT
cost.impact: BigQuery bills for bytes scanned regardless of rows returned. An exploratory query without LIMIT on a large table incurs full scan cost even if you only need a sample of the data.
cost.fix_guidance: Add LIMIT for exploratory queries: SELECT * FROM t LIMIT 1000. Use table previews in the console for sampling. Use _PARTITIONTIME or _PARTITIONDATE filters to limit scan range.

cost.name: SELECT * Wastes Snowflake Credits
cost.impact: Snowflake stores data in columnar micro-partitions. SELECT * prevents column pruning and forces scanning all partitions. On large tables this multiplies credit consumption and increases query time, directly increasing warehouse cost.
cost.fix_guidance: Select only needed columns. Use clustering keys on large tables. Consider materialized views for frequently queried column subsets.

cost.name: LATERAL FLATTEN Without Warehouse Consideration
cost.description: FLATTEN on large VARIANT arrays may need larger warehouse to avoid spilling.
cost.impact: Undersized warehouse causes disk spilling, multiplying credit consumption.
cost.fix_guidance: Monitor query profile for spilling. Scale warehouse for heavy FLATTEN operations.

cost.name: Repeated Subquery Instead of CTE
cost.description: Repeating the same subquery scans data multiple times — use CTEs.
cost.impact: Each repeated subquery scans underlying tables again, multiplying cost.
cost.fix_guidance: Extract into WITH (CTE) clause. BigQuery materializes CTEs referenced multiple times.

cost.name: UNLOAD Without PARALLEL Consideration
cost.description: Redshift UNLOAD defaults to PARALLEL ON which creates multiple files. For small result sets, PARALLEL OFF creates a single file and avoids S3 request overhead.
cost.impact: Default PARALLEL ON creates many small S3 files. For downstream consumers expecting a single file, this requires extra processing.
cost.fix_guidance: Add PARALLEL OFF for single-file output (small results) or PARALLEL ON MAXFILESIZE for controlled output (large results).

cost.name: SELECT * on ClickHouse Columnar Storage
cost.description: ClickHouse MergeTree stores data in columnar format. SELECT * reads every column from disk even if only a few are needed, wasting I/O bandwidth and decompression CPU.
cost.impact: ClickHouse column pruning is one of its key optimizations. SELECT * bypasses this, reading and decompressing every column.
cost.fix_guidance: Always specify explicit column names. ClickHouse decompresses only requested columns, dramatically reducing I/O.

cost.name: SELECT * on Partitioned Hive Table
cost.description: SELECT * on partitioned Hive tables in Presto/Trino reads all partitions and all columns. Always filter on partition columns and specify needed columns to minimize data scanned.
cost.impact: Hive tables can have thousands of partitions and hundreds of columns. SELECT * reads everything, multiplying I/O and cost.
cost.fix_guidance: Specify columns explicitly. Add WHERE on partition columns: WHERE dt = '2024-01-01'. Use SHOW PARTITIONS to understand layout.

cost.name: Full Scan Without Partition Filter
cost.description: Queries on partitioned Delta/Hive tables without a WHERE clause on the partition column read all partitions, multiplying I/O and compute cost.
cost.impact: A partitioned table with 365 daily partitions will read 365x more data without a partition filter. Databricks charges per DBU.
cost.fix_guidance: Always filter on partition columns: WHERE date = '2024-01-01'. Enable spark.sql.sources.partitionOverwriteMode=dynamic for safe overwrites.

cost.name: Query Cache Pollution
cost.description: Large analytical queries in MySQL can pollute the query cache, evicting frequently-used cached results. Use SQL_NO_CACHE for one-off or analytical queries.
cost.impact: Large result sets evict smaller, frequently-used entries from the query cache, degrading performance for other queries.
cost.fix_guidance: Add SQL_NO_CACHE: SELECT SQL_NO_CACHE ... for analytical or one-off queries. Note: query cache is removed in MySQL 8.0+.

cost.name: Cursor Without FAST_FORWARD
cost.description: T-SQL cursors default to GLOBAL KEYSET which maintains a copy of keys in tempdb. FAST_FORWARD (forward-only, read-only) is the most efficient cursor type and avoids tempdb overhead.
cost.impact: Non-FAST_FORWARD cursors maintain key sets in tempdb, consuming I/O and storage. For read-only forward iteration this is waste.
cost.fix_guidance: Use DECLARE cur CURSOR FAST_FORWARD FOR SELECT ... Or better: replace the cursor with a set-based operation.

cost.name: Full Table Scan Hint
cost.description: The /*+ FULL(table) */ hint forces Oracle to perform a full table scan, bypassing all indexes. This is rarely appropriate in OLTP and wastes I/O.
cost.impact: Forces reading every block in the table regardless of available indexes. On large tables this is orders of magnitude slower.
cost.fix_guidance: Remove the FULL hint and let the optimizer choose. If the optimizer makes poor choices, update statistics with DBMS_STATS.GATHER_TABLE_STATS.

cost.name: CACHE TABLE Without Filter
cost.description: CACHE TABLE without a filter caches the entire table in memory. For large tables this wastes executor memory and may cause OOM or evict more useful cached data.
cost.impact: Caching a 100GB table consumes 100GB of executor memory across the cluster. This evicts other cached data and may cause OOM.
cost.fix_guidance: Cache only needed partitions: CACHE TABLE t OPTIONS ('partitionFilter' = "dt = '2024-01-01'"). Or use CACHE LAZY TABLE to defer until first access.
